title: CatBoost categorical diversity
summary: CatBoost is designed for categorical features through ordered target statistics and ordered boosting, reducing prediction shift from categorical leakage.
rationale: CatBoost is designed for categorical features through ordered target statistics and ordered boosting, reducing prediction shift from categorical leakage. The existing best uses AutoGluon GBM/XGB only, so CatBoost can provide orthogonal rankings for Driver/Race/Compound combinations.
implementation_hint: Train a standalone CatBoostClassifier with native categorical columns plus engineered categorical crosses, eval_metric='AUC', class_weights or scale_pos_weight if imbalanced, early stopping, and multiple seeds. Blend its rank-normalized predictions with the best GBM/XGB predictions.
expected_effect: Improves ensemble diversity and high-cardinality categorical handling without extensive manual one-hot encoding.
risk: CatBoost can underperform if categorical crosses are too sparse or validation split is random; its gain is most likely through blending, not replacement.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
